lifecycle.proof_status: PASS_MT002_2026-07-01: cargo test --test model_lane_schema_pg_tests --features test-utils = 4 passed / 0 failed (managed PG 127.0.0.1:5544, 351s); commit e083e0d2. Prior FALSE self-cert corrected. FIXES (spec-grounded, cloud gate NOT weakened): (A/C) schema test now seeds durable cloud ProjectionPlan+ConsentReceipt via new shared tests/model_lane_cloud_support.rs::seed_cloud_lane_authority (spec 4.3.9.2.5 confirms the fail-closed durability gate is correct); (B) CODE bug fixed - record_lane_terminal_status re-stamps the terminal EventLedger payload with the full lane record for replay authority; record_message idempotency now normalizes only per-attempt message_id/span_id while still failing closed on authority/routing divergence (spec 4.3.9.2.5 idempotent-retry); adjacent revoke_cloud_consent_receipt same re-stamp (MT-006 code in MT-002 allowed path, disclosed, prevents MT-006 revocation regression - cloud_consent_revocation test PASS). KB-reviewed the 3 model_lane.rs hunks: correct, not scaffolding. Shared cloud-seed helper reusable by MT-004/005/006/007/009.
lifecycle.last_runtime_status_ref: Implemented Rust PostgreSQL/EventLedger ModelLane storage, Dexterity SwarmCoordinator launch recording, fail-closed validators, UserManual coverage, and exact proof commands.

scope.summary: Add backend schemas/types for ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, and lane status.
forbidden_paths: cloud provider direct-call code outside existing lane boundaries
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Types encode run id, lane id, lane kind, backend, role, session id, status, WP/MT refs, message kind, payload ref, summary, and authority state.
Serde roundtrip tests cover local, cloud, CLI, human, subagent, and validator lanes.
Message payload refs are required for non-status payloads.

ModelLaneRun and every side-effecting ModelLane MUST carry a validated Locus binding: work_packet_id/wp_id, micro_task_id/mt_id, task_board_id when present, coordinator_session_id, session_id/model_session_id, and owner_session. Missing, invalid, or mismatched binding against the active WP/MT contract MUST fail closed with diagnostics and Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence.

ModelLane MUST include adapter_id, runtime_binding, provider_kind, capability_token_ids or effective_capability_snapshot_ref, and tool_gate_decision_refs; any lane tool call without session-scoped capability intersection MUST fail closed.

ModelLaneMessage MUST include idempotency_key, event_ledger_ref or event_ledger_seq, payload_sha256, proposal_ref when kind=PROPOSAL, base_snapshot_ref/state_vector when the payload targets shared editable state, and replay_order_key. Duplicate retries with the same idempotency_key and payload hash MUST be idempotent; same key with different payload hash MUST fail closed.

Schemas carry trace_id, run_span_id/lane_span_id/message_span_id, parent_span_id or linked_span_contexts, model_session_id/session_id, wp_id, mt_id, Locus work-unit refs, and EventLedger aggregate/event refs so every lane can be joined across Flight Recorder, Locus, and replay surfaces.

Schemas MUST carry deterministic EventLedger replay ordering fields: event_ledger_stream_id, event_ledger_append_id or event_ledger_event_id, event_ledger_seq, event_stream_version, transaction_seq, replay_order_key, and replay_after_event_ledger_seq. created_at_utc MUST NOT be the ordering authority.

Promoted ModelLaneMessage records MUST carry promotion_gate_ref, promotion_receipt_ref, validator_verdict_ref or operator_decision_ref as applicable, plus promoted_artifact_ref, promoted_artifact_sha256, and promoted_artifact_version when authority artifacts are created or updated.

CRDT-targeting ModelLaneMessage records MUST carry crdt_update_ref, crdt_base_snapshot_ref, crdt_state_vector, crdt_proposal_ref, and crdt_stale_base_ref as applicable; missing CRDT base/proposal metadata for shared editable state MUST fail closed before promotion.

ModelLane recovery fields MUST include recovery_state, heartbeat_at_utc, lease_expires_at_utc, reclaim_after_utc, restart_generation, cancellation_ref, backpressure_ref, loop_counter_ref, last_runtime_status_ref, last_recovery_event_ref, failstate_code, reason_ref, and recovery_hint_ref.

Side-effecting ModelLaneMessage records MUST carry immutable Locus binding fields on the message itself: coordinator_session_id, task_board_id when present, work_packet_id/wp_id, micro_task_id/mt_id, owner_session, and locus_binding_ref.

Every ModelLaneRun invocation records FEMS posture: memory_pack_ref, memory_pack_hash, determinism_mode, budget summary, selected/candidate ids for replay mode, procedural-review status, truncation warnings, and exclusion/rejection reasons.

Implement canonical Rust/PostgreSQL model-lane aggregate storage for ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, schema registry rows, and EventLedger append/list/replay APIs; structs-only or in-memory-only schema work is not acceptable.

Negative proof MUST cover missing Locus binding, idempotency-key conflict with different payload hash, missing payload_sha256, malformed trace/span linkage, and unsupported provider capability snapshot.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: Later routing can only pass opaque prompt text and cannot recover lane state.

implementation_notes: Implement in Rust backend types, preferably under swarm_orchestration with no front-end-owned authority.
ModelLaneRun must include run_id, coordinator_session_id, routing_policy, context_bundle_id, lane_ids, event_ledger_scope, artifact_namespace, created_at, updated_at, and terminal status.

ModelLane must include lane_id, run_id, kind, role, backend, model_id, session_id/model_session_id correlation, launch_authority, status, wp_id, mt_id, owner_session, started_at, finished_at, cancellation token if applicable, and recovery_state.

ModelLaneMessage must include message_id, run_id, from_lane_id, to_lane_id, kind, payload_ref, summary, typed routing fields target_role/target_session/correlation_id/requires_ack/ack_for when cross-agent, authority state, created_at, and error code when failed.
Persist durable lane/message records through PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore-compatible boundaries; SQLite is not allowed for runtime, cache, fixture, fallback, or test authority.

Provider capabilities from the folded provider stub must be represented: streaming, tool_calling, structured_output, multi_turn, strict fail-closed missing feature behavior, and adapter-only provider quirks.
Layerwise/dynamic compute folded scope must be representable as requested_exec_policy/effective_exec_policy/waiver_ref/trace_artifact_ref fields without implementing true layer-wise algorithms.
ModelLane records must be EventLedger/PostgreSQL aggregates with typed state transitions, not only in-memory coordinator structs.
For shared editable surfaces, lane message schemas must distinguish advisory model output, CRDT draft/proposal, validation result, denial, and promoted authority state.
Use UUID v7 or existing project ID helpers consistently where product code already requires time-ordered IDs.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: A loose enum or freeform JSON shape will drift across adapters.
minimum_controls: Use typed structs and explicit enums with negative deserialization tests.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage schemas must carry replayable IDs, trace/span correlation, adapter/capability/tool-gate fields, Locus WP/MT/session bindings, FEMS posture, and CRDT state-vector/proposal metadata where shared state is touched.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
